description_long: This object consists of two wooden barrels and two wooden crates, all arranged side by side. The barrels are cylindrical with visible wood grain textures and reinforced by metal hoops, one with a darker, more polished finish and the other with a weathered, grayish appearance. The crates are cubic, constructed from wooden planks with visible slats and corner reinforcements, one lighter and one darker in tone. The barrels have open tops, suggesting they can be used for storage or transport of goods, while the crates appear to be closed. The wood surfaces show a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of utilitarian storage containers found in urban or industrial settings. No visible handles or lids are present, and there are no labels or markings on any of the objects.
description: A set of two wooden barrels with metal hoops and two wooden crates, all with visible wood grain and a utilitarian, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing both barrels and crates side by side, highlighting the wood grain and metal hoops on the barrels.
description_view_1: Side view with the barrels aligned behind the crates, showing the rounded profile of the barrels and the square faces of the crates.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the barrels and crates visible from the opposite side, emphasizing the texture and construction.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, with the barrels behind the crates, showing the alternate faces and reinforcing the cylindrical and cubic forms.
